claim: MLX enables on-device deployment of large models on Apple Silicon; 4000+ models ported, day-zero support for frontier open-source models like Gemma 4
actionable: When deploying LLMs on Apple devices, use MLX framework to access latest models; check Hugging Face for MLX-optimized versions to get day-zero support
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Turbo Quant reduces KV cache by 4x and achieves 1M token context on device; throughput improves 2x at 300K context compared to full model
actionable: For long-context on-device inference, apply Turbo Quant quantization to reduce memory consumption by 4x while maintaining model quality
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Speech-to-speech models generate audio in under 100ms on device; custom model Marvis achieves real-time audio generation on Apple hardware
actionable: When building voice-enabled on-device applications, use MLX Audio with Marvis for sub-100ms speech-to-speech latency
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: On-device small models with tool access outperform large models for agentic tasks; knowledge gaps bridged by reliable tool use rather than memorization
actionable: For on-device agent deployment, prioritize reasoning capability and tool use over memorized knowledge; pair small models with search/API tools
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Real-time object detection and background blur run at 60+ fps on device using MLX VLM; completely offline operation verified by testing with internet disabled
actionable: For privacy-sensitive vision tasks, deploy vision models via MLX VLM for real-time on-device processing without cloud connectivity
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Gemma 4 26B with hundreds of billions of parameters can run on M1 MacBook using MLX storage-aware inference with reasonable speeds
actionable: Use storage-aware inference techniques to run very large models on memory-constrained devices by leveraging disk storage as extended VRAM
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Cloud assumption fails for unreliable connectivity scenarios; on-device deployment critical for robotics, offline access, and regulated environments
actionable: For deployments in regions with unreliable internet or regulated industries, design on-device AI systems rather than relying on cloud connectivity
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs

claim: Video generation on 16GB VRAM produces coherent multi-shot stories by chaining generations; demonstrated with MLX video framework on device
actionable: For narrative video generation, chain multiple video generation calls with shared context to create coherent multi-scene stories on limited VRAM
source_title: Why MLX — Prince Canuma, Neywa Labs
